title: Learning the A Matrix from Observations

runtime.expected_outcome: Over repeated episodes with learning_enabled=true, the A-matrix concentration parameters accumulate and the likelihood sharpens toward the empirical distribution.

symbols.A_{i,j}: observation-model entry derived from a via normalisation

audiences.kid: Every time you see a clue at a spot, you add a tally. The spot's clue-count tells you what clue to expect next time.
audiences.real: DirichletUpdateA bumps per-column concentration parameters. Over episodes, the A matrix approaches the true observation statistics.
audiences.equation: a' = a + sum_t q(s_t) o_t^T (outer product). Normalise columns to get A. Prior concentration controls learning rate.
audiences.derivation: Dirichlet-categorical conjugacy: observations are counts over categories, prior is Dirichlet(a), posterior is Dirichlet(a + counts). In active inference this is the structure-learning step (Friston et al. 2016).

orchestrate.objective: Show A-matrix learning across episodes using the existing DirichletUpdateA action.
orchestrate.context: corridor_turns, learning_enabled=true; run 5+ episodes and log the A-matrix column norms.

credits: Parr, Pezzulo & Friston (2022), Active Inference, MIT Press (CC BY-NC-ND), Ch 7.
Friston, FitzGerald, Rigoli, Schwartenbeck, O'Doherty & Pezzulo (2016), Active inference and learning.
